families.name: Direkt detaljhämtning

trap_anatomy.exemplar_why_tempting: It's easy to read 'Energizing melodies tend to boost our mood in general, waking us up if we are feeling tired' as 'only cheerful music helps when you're down'.
Left-to-right reading might land on the phrase 'people whose ability to pick up emotion-laden social cues, such as facial expressions or tone of voice, is severely compromised' and
If you skim 'intelligence is not a substance at all' as a quote attributed to Gardner, you might invert it and read 'Gardner found intelligence is one substance'.
First instinct is to fill in a story-shaped explanation: shortage of GPs because they're going to higher-paid specialties — a plausible real-world cause.
First instinct on resistance to a new medical idea is to attribute it to medical professionals defending their turf.

layer1_distractor_patterns: Sant påstående från fel stycke
Allmänkunskap som inte står i texten
Kvantifikator-uppgradering (some → all, often → always)
Orsakssamband påklistrat på beskrivande utsaga
families.sample_technique: The past-mistake / present-fix arc: short ELF texts often pivot on one word ('Now', 'Today', 'Recently'). Find that pivot and the correct option is almost always the one that names the lesson learned.
families.difficulty_spread: unknown (no item-level ratings exported from production DB)
families.notes: Känns igen på frågeverben: 'we are told', 'is said', 'is stated', 'according to the text' UTAN 'main' eller 'implied'. ~101 frågor i korpus — den största enskilda kategorin. Lägst svårighetsgrad när man läser noggrant; hög svårighet bara när passagen innehåller flera liknande-klingande stycken.

families.name: Gapped text (cloze) — banked gap fill

trap_anatomy.exemplar_why_tempting: 'Soft' is a natural-sounding everyday adjective, and English learners may translate from a native-language collocation.
Many associate food-tax discussions with disappointment about public health and reach for 'Unfortunately' as a tonal fit.
Left-to-right reading might leap from 'quality of life declines' to social consequences like dislike or rejection.
If you read 'Scientists have known' as a piece of conditional knowledge — what they know depends on circumstances — 'unless' might creep in.
It's easy to read 'observations out in the real world' as a STRENGTH — real-world data feels more authentic — and pick 'superior'.

families.sample_technique: On cloze adjectives, read TWO sentences past the gap — the author usually spells out the consequence (here: misdiagnosis), and that consequence fixes the missing adjective's meaning.
families.difficulty_spread: unknown (no item-level ratings exported from production DB)
families.notes: Format family, not a cognitive-op family: one ~355-word text with 5 numbered gaps (q31–35 of the second verbal pass in every sitting). Prompts are empty; each gap has 4 alternatives. Tests collocation, discourse polarity (but/yet/however), register fit, and referential cohesion rather than passage comprehension.

families.name: Slutsatsdragning / implikation

trap_anatomy.exemplar_why_tempting: If you read 'casual or portfolio workers' as 'part-time workers in general', B feels close — the passage does say the present system hurts certain workers.
Many stop at the phrase 'reality TV' and assume the author looks down on it — so dismissive viewer attitude feels in keeping.
First instinct: a critical paragraph about a public institution implies public anger.
Snap reading swaps the analogy's source for its target: 'drugs have side-effects, so drugs need more control'.
Many stop at the alarming-feeling 'Roman medicines also made use of lead compounds' and project that the Romans must have been ignorant of the dangers.

layer1_distractor_patterns: Bokstavlig omformulering av passagen
Två-stegs-hopp (rimligt men ostött)
Inferens i omvänd riktning
Omfångsskifte (specifikt fall → generell princip)
families.sample_technique: Implication-question items: when a passage describes a flaw without proposing a fix, the correct option usually names the unspoken fix at the system level ('will have to be revised'). Specifics-only options miss the implied step.
families.difficulty_spread: unknown (no item-level ratings exported from production DB)
families.notes: Känns igen på 'implied' (~56 frågor i korpus). Persona-passet flaggade detta som hög-fel-typen när prosan är tät och akademisk. Kalibreringsregel: om alternativet förekommer ordagrant i passagen är det INTE implikations-svaret.

families.name: Huvudidé / argument / fokus

trap_anatomy.exemplar_why_tempting: Left-to-right reading lands on 'researchers assessed their physical activity' and 'cognition' and you fill in a familiar phrase like 'short-term memory'.
Many stop at 'piety and sexual restraint' in the last paragraph and read it as the writer endorsing a return to those values.
Many stop at 'transform itself' and conclude that something is going wrong — maybe forensic evidence is being dismissed when it shouldn't be.
It's tempting to read 'forensic science has to transform itself' and 'a change is already under way' as evidence that the SCIENCE has been extended — progress narrative.
Many stop at 'rate has tripled' and conclude that better screening tools must have uncovered cases that used to be missed — a common real-world hypothesis.

layer1_distractor_patterns: Stöddetalj klädd som huvudpunkt
Slogan som skjuter över textens omfång
Ämne vs tes-förväxling
Motposition som författaren nämner men förkastar
families.sample_technique: The verdict-sentence rule: paragraphs that describe research usually save their thesis for the last sentence. Read it first when the prompt asks for the main point — the rest is method, the closer is the message.
families.difficulty_spread: unknown (no item-level ratings exported from production DB)
families.notes: Känns igen på 'main point / main argument / main claim / main focus / main conclusion / main impression' (~43 frågor i korpus, plus några 'main purpose'). Hög-hävstångs-typ — toppelever klarar dessa genom att ankra i tesmeningen istället för minnesvärda detaljer.

families.name: Hållning / ton / attityd

trap_anatomy.exemplar_why_tempting: It's tempting to read 'inclusive and nuanced' as 'complicated' and conclude non-specialists will struggle — nuance can sound forbidding.
Many stop at 'fatal impact' and the discussion of colonisation, headline 'Trouble in Paradise' — those feel grim, so the book might come across as depressing.
It's easy to read the Lucknow rejection ('petition was turned down… died from starvation') and generalise: the British 'usually' refused responsibility.
It's easy to read the opening statistic (99% male, 4% even by booking app) as the writer warning about a hostile climate toward women drivers and to assume A captures the concern.
Many stop at 'other narratives of exploitation and trade are ignored' and read the focus as too narrow for big conclusions.

layer1_distractor_patterns: Ämnets ton förväxlad med författarens ton
En grad för starkt
Blandad hållning plattad till en pol
Lånad från en citerad källa
families.sample_technique: On 'summarise the reviewer's impression' items, scan the LAST paragraph for the reviewer's verdict — usually a pair of praises or critiques. Pick the option that names BOTH halves of the verdict; options that catch only one half (or reverse one half) are the typical traps.
families.difficulty_spread: unknown (no item-level ratings exported from production DB)
families.notes: Känns igen på 'tone', 'attitude', 'characterized', 'best be described', 'reviewer's' / 'writer's overall' (~23 frågor i korpus). Trajektorian taggade detta som en hög-fel-typ även i v3 — fina distinktioner mellan t.ex. 'on the whole positive but has some serious objections' vs 'clearly impressed but avoids taking a stand' är det envisa felmönstret.

families.name: Logisk slutsats / syntes

trap_anatomy.exemplar_why_tempting: First instinct on 'highlight the need to think broadly' is to read it as a call to fix the problem entirely with better research.
Many stop at 'scholastic pecking order' and read it as the author endorsing ranking as common and reliable.
It's easy to read the Macintyre praise as a recommendation for both books.
It's easy to read 'untold value in knowing a little about a lot' as a victory lap for common sense over expert knowledge — as if specialists are being replaced.

layer1_distractor_patterns: Slutsats dragen enbart från sista meningen
Slutsats som överdriver en gardera premiss
Halv-rätt slutsats som saknar en gren
Slutsats lånad från ett annat stycke
families.sample_technique: First-paragraph items: the headline is usually the second-to-last sentence (the implicit goal) plus the last sentence (the failure mode). Use both. Here: writers SHOULD overturn intuitions, but OFTEN reinforce them — so explaining is hard.
families.difficulty_spread: unknown (no item-level ratings exported from production DB)
families.notes: Känns igen på 'can be concluded' / 'may be concluded' (~32 frågor i korpus). Skiljer sig från ELF-TYPE-002 (inferens) genom att slutsatsen syntetiserar EXPLICITA utsagor; inferens fyller i osagda bryggor. Skiljer sig från ELF-TYPE-004 (huvudidé) genom att slutsatsen riktas mot ett NAMNGIVET STYCKE, inte hela textens tes.

families.name: Påstående-text-överensstämmelse

trap_anatomy.exemplar_why_tempting: If you bring your own picture of Cleopatra to the passage, 'beautiful and imaginative' feels like a natural description of her popular image.
Many stop at the sentence 'we invent fictions to justify and rationalise' subconscious choices and read it as a knock against the value of storytelling.
It's easy to read the praise-heavy opening paragraph and infer the reviewer thinks the research is excellent across the board.
It's easy to mix up who said what — 'Storms and other disturbances … seem to be more important than sea level' sounds like a Duvat-style claim if you skim.
Quick scans land here when you read 'Pietilä appears as a character called Too-Ticky' and assume every Moominland figure must have a real-world model.

layer1_distractor_patterns: Sant i verkligheten men inte i texten
Stött av texten men med fel omfång
Rimlig förlängning som texten inte gör
Inverterad polaritet
families.sample_technique: Agreement-check items ('most in line with the text'): find a sentence in the passage that is repeated in different words — universality phrasing here appears in three forms (across cultures, across abilities, same-room similarity). Whichever option restates that repeated idea in plain English wins.
families.difficulty_spread: unknown (no item-level ratings exported from production DB)
families.notes: Känns igen på 'which of the following statements is most in line with' / 'in agreement with' / 'is true according to' (~24 frågor i korpus). Skiljer sig från ELF-TYPE-001 retrieval genom att frågan INTE namnger vad man ska slå upp — du måste låta alternativen styra scanningen. Tidskrävande typ; budgetera upp till 4 uppslag per fråga.

families.name: (untagged residual)

trap_anatomy.exemplar_why_tempting: Left-to-right reading of 'cultural difference was negotiated' might land on 'difference' and 'negotiated' as signs of friction — friction reads as suspicion.
It's tempting to read 'climate change' in the passage and reach for the modern association — humans causing the change.
The passage spends a paragraph on birds and chimps, so 'similar to certain types of animal language' feels like a continuation of that thread.

families.sample_technique: On 'relationship between X and Y' items, locate the author's view of the CONTACT itself (the verb that describes what happens between X and Y — interactive, exploitative, hostile, neutral). The option that mirrors that verb is the answer.
families.difficulty_spread: unknown (no item-level ratings exported from production DB)

families.name: Syfte / orsak-verkan

trap_anatomy.exemplar_why_tempting: It's easy to project a personality stereotype — gym-goers are impatient, classical is slow — onto the question.
First instinct on dramatic phrases like 'frantically devising strategies' is that the author is criticising the kings.
Once you see 'bizarre to claim', it's tempting to escalate to 'consistent disregard of historical facts'.

layer1_distractor_patterns: Rimlig verklig orsak som inte står i texten
Konsekvens etiketterad som orsak
Syfte kollapsat till ämne
Lokal orsak när texten ramar in en djupare
families.sample_technique: Cause-question items ('Why, according to the text…'): the answer is the mechanism in the passage, not a plausible-sounding cultural explanation. Look for body-or-brain language ('arousal', 'response', 'system') and match it to the option that names the same mechanism.
families.difficulty_spread: unknown (no item-level ratings exported from production DB)
families.notes: Känns igen på 'why', 'main reason', 'purpose', 'caused', 'why is X mentioned' (~19 frågor i korpus totalt). Två underkategorier delar attack-protokoll eftersom båda frågar 'identifiera författarens explicita tillskrivning'. Liten kategori men distinkt nog att hålla separat — syfte/orsak är inte utbytbara med retrieval (ingen ytfras att matcha) eller inferens (texten namnger orsaken).

families.name: Ordförråd / uttryck i kontext

trap_anatomy.exemplar_why_tempting: If you read 'still ran high' as 'a filter still operated', A can sound like a plausible screening mechanism — only certain personalities allowed in.
If you read 'still ran high' as 'a filter still operated', A can sound like a plausible screening mechanism — only certain personalities allowed in.
'Modifying genes of crops, animals and even humans' has an ominous tone, and pairing 'growth of millionaires' with massive inequality could feel unethical.

layer1_distractor_patterns: Bokstavlig läsning av det bildliga uttrycket
Standard idiombetydelse vs textens böjda användning
Närliggande-koncept-substitution
families.sample_technique: On vocabulary-in-context questions with a TWO-PART phrase ('X as well as Y'), split it in half and translate each half separately. Then pick the option where BOTH halves match — not just one. This catches options that ace one half and fumble the other.
families.difficulty_spread: unknown (no item-level ratings exported from production DB)
families.notes: Känns igen på citerat uttryck i frågan + verben 'best corresponds to', 'is said about the expression', 'is implied about the expression', 'reflects'. Bara 5 förekomster i korpus — på golvet för en typ. Flaggad för v2: expandera bara om korpus växer; annars överväg att vika in i ELF-TYPE-001 eller ELF-TYPE-002 efter kontext.
